title: Logic Gates and Boolean Algebra
summary: Understand the electronic gates used to build digital circuits and the Boolean algebra that describes them.
content_html:
Logic gates are electronic components whose output is determined by their inputs. They implement Boolean algebra, where variables are either 0 (false) or 1 (true).

Basic Gates

GateSymbolExpressionRule
NOT▷○c = a'Inverts the input
ANDD-shapec = a.b1 only when ALL inputs are 1
ORShieldc = a+b1 when ANY input is 1
XORShield+c = a⊕b1 when inputs are DIFFERENT
NANDAND+○c = (a.b)'Opposite of AND
NOROR+○c = (a+b)'Opposite of OR

Universal Gate

NAND is a universal gate — any Boolean function can be built using only NAND gates (discovered by Charles Sanders Peirce).

De Morgan's Laws

(a.b)' = a' + b'    (NAND law)
(a+b)' = a' . b'    (NOR law)

Boolean Arithmetic Examples

1 AND 1 = 1,  1 AND 0 = 0
1 OR  0 = 1,  0 OR  0 = 0
1 XOR 1 = 0,  1 XOR 0 = 1
NOT 1 = 0,   NOT 0 = 1

options: Inputs are different from each other
exercises.answer: Inputs are different from each other
